tracks.text: lyrics for "innocent arrogance" by BiSH, Are you ready? I'm ready.
A heart that you can't understand just by looking at
Are you ready? One more time
If we can't choose our future, then what can we choose?
Emotions, Delusions
They don't make sense anymore. This crumbling city smirks.
Nothing is certain
There's no way to understand, but I still want to be certain.
Because I won't show the things I don't want to show, I can't find the things that are important.
I can't stay pure, right?
Let us, let us, move on
Takao sits in a wheelchair. Tokio stands with him in front of stones across water.
He pulls his sleeve over a dark bruise emerging from bandages
Sliced black characters on a white background read, Two Confessions
Two girls kiss under a tree. Tokio stares. The girls notice her, smile, then run off together. She watches. Her fingers rise to her lips.
Kuku stands behind her
Kuku hangs from a 10 foot branch like a monkey, holding the 8 armed fish drawing. Tokio walks away dejectedly. Mimihime watches Kona draw. Tokio pulls her out her seat and she rolls across the grass.
The white haired classmate watches through a window. He walks out of a bathroom, drying his hair with a towel. In his room, a desk hosts bolts, cables, and a solder gun.

He opens it, hides it while looking around, then gazes back on the screen, blushing. In the picture, Mimihime stands in a shower, eyes closed, her arms raised to her hair. Water pours on to her nude body, as soap clings to her curves. Shot from shoulder height, she seems unaware of the camera.\

He opens it, hides it while looking around, then gazes back on the screen, blushing. In the picture, Mimihime stands in a shower, eyes closed, arms raised over her head, washing her hair. The water pours over her nude body, as soap clings in white, frothy strands over her curves.

Tokio tapes a colored picture to the wall of her room. It depicts a white ball with teeth. She sits on her bed and stares at the other pictures, including whales flying over trees. Elsewhere, Kiruko's eyes flutter open in the dark ryokan room.
Kiruko sits up in her futon, back to the window, where eyeballs press against the glass. She eyes her gun.
She turns to see an empty window.
Kiruko and Maru stumble through the dark outside
The innkeeper walks in front of the house, gun in hand, looking around the darkness. She stops and turns to see Maru holding a flashlight.
A shadow flies across the sky and ducks behind a rusted car. The teens approach as the clouds pass over the moon.
The 2 train their flashlights on the car. They slowly search around.
A blue, glowing symbol flickers behind Kiruko
She turns around. A blue star symbol glows from the disconnected neck of the monster.
The bucket shatters
Silver strands

The monster charges to them, whips striking, driving sparks in all directions. Kiruko leaps to the side, pushing Maru to the ground. The whips hit the car. The monster's neck turns as the car explodes.
Kiruko and Maru press themselves to the ground behind a hill.
It drops down from above, its wings burning. They run. It chases
They turn right and jump a wall. The monster crashes through.
The innkeeper turns towards the noise. She grips her rifle.
Moonlight begins to illuminate the dirt road
The innkeeper crouches behind a bush

The monster pokes its neck out and looks down the road. It turns towards a light, held by Maru, standing next to a truck. Kiruko crouches behind the truck. Maru flickers the light. The monster stalks towards him. Kiruko sneaks towards the front of the truck, eyeing the monster from behind. She puts her finger on the trigger of the gun. From below, the monster resembles a a giant chicken, its knees even with truck's steering wheel.
The innkeeper fingers the trigger of her rifle
The innkeeper pushes Kiruko to the ground. Her bullet cuts through the left arm, leaving a second stump.
She stands between them and the monster
She reaches out her hands to the trembling, injured monster.
Whips remove the top half of her head, the chop through her torso.
The monster's neck oozes towards the chunks of corpse, eyes and teeth emerging from the ichor. Maru looks away then back, in time to see the monster sucker onto the innkeeper's head, eyes bulging.
He pulls her to face him as a tear runs down her cheek. He wipes it away.
Maru walks confidently forward. He pauses as a whip slices inches from his foot. He stares at the monster. The monster stares at him.
In slow motion, Maru darts forward as the monster leaps back. Whips fly out. Maru dodges left, down, right. He reaches out a hand and grabs the monster's leg.
The monster flails. Maru concentrates, holding his hand against its body.

We zoom in through his hand, into the monster, through red fluid with floating blood cells. Golden light tears through the red, soupy abyss, forming a hand that reaches out and grabs of lump of throbbing red flesh. It squeezes, and the lump explodes. Outside, the monster flails and screams.
The monster's neck rears up and bolts, strands of ichor growing and reforming and disconnecting until it geysers black, sticky blood
During the day, the battery pack charges in the ryokan. A picture depicts Yuuto on his 7th birthday
Maru looks at the picture of Yuuto
She smiles softly and pats his head. He blushes.
Grass grows over train tracks. Kiruko brushes her teeth.
The tracks lead over a ridge, where standing water has claimed a former valley town.
A board labelled Kiruko Maru lowers by rope down to the water. The pair look down at it from the roof of a high rise.
Carrying makeshift paddles, the pair climb emerge from a stair well and wade into the water. Kiruko jumps onto the raft made of boards taped and tied to empty gasoline cans and other plastic.
She sweats and averts her gaze
They paddle under an overpass
Bulging red tomatoes dangle from vines under glowing hexagons
A ball rolls to a door. Tokio pauses, looking up at a camera
The security camera reads 17/06/23 11:18:33. Tokio watches the camera as she walks over, picks up the ball, and slowly backs away.
She runs into a floor robot
The black haired boy places 2 small, box-y robots against a pillar. He climbs on top of them. Their screens display an up arrow, and they slowly rise. Several other students watch.
He rises above the trees. The screens flash the word offline, and then drop from the pillar. He falls. The other students jump. Mimihime hides her face in the white haired boy's chest.
The boy gets up. Mimihime sighs and lets go of the white hair boy, who blushes. Blood drips from his nose.
Sign reads, Kusakabe Farm, Trespassers will be mercilessly killed
A pocket sized metal case. Kiruko removes a brass cylinder.
Flashback, Kiruko pulls back a blanket, revealing dark bruises spotting the arms and legs of Miruka, a 20-something woman with black hair.
A man lifts a makeshift gate. He leads them along a road past fields where another man hoes rows in the ground and children play with sticks. A woman waters marijuana plants.
An old man smokes a blunt. Another man plays guitar. A woman dances in her underwear.
Kiruko with black hair
The race fan swings his flashlight around, revealing box with a red seal of a bird and the moon.
Top of grip, left side
She clasps his hands
Later, the pair stand together
A crescent moon shines over the calm, open ocean
A ferry drifts through the night
He leans in. She looks at him. He closes his eyes and leans. She falls back. He follows.
Lyrics, No one else, Nowhere else, knows anything about this story of going to heaven
The bonfire has been put out then the sky is colourful
Hey, let's start treasure hunting
Push the debris and head to paradise
Finding myself
Hands together, we debut once more.
This future you believe in? I will protect it.
Search for the reason and move forward into the illuminated world now
Reaching my hands to make it happen
Seeking the reason, and once my hand reach the heart
To the end of the sky together
why worry about the ringing voice
the scarlet light shines
Why ask for, I am going to keep this promise, so
Let's run further than the dream
Let's run to heaven
Credits roll.

Post-episode footnote: Traditional Japanese inns are called ryokan. In the last 2 episodes, the inn was called Hachisu Ryokan, spelled 8 + nest + travel + building. The word hachisu could mean beehive, but this hotel used the character for 8 instead of bee. Both are pronounced hachi. The second character, su, can mean any home for an animal, like nest, hive, den, web, or rookery. Incidentally, another name invoked raising young. The yakuza family name, NawaShiro, spelled seedling + change, means nursery.
